title: Matrix Integration
summary: Matrix protocol integration via the mautrix SDK, covering account registration, E2EE with libolm, crypto store setup, cross-signing recovery key, proxy mode for macOS, MSC3245 voice messages, and room allowlisting.
key_concepts: Crypto store for persistent encryption keys
Cross-signing recovery key for key verification
Proxy mode for macOS (libolm not available as native binary)
Bot account registration on Matrix homeserver
Sync token persistence for resuming after restart

env_vars.name: MATRIX_HOMESERVER
env_vars.description: Matrix homeserver URL (e.g. https://matrix.org or self-hosted Synapse)

env_vars.name: MATRIX_ACCESS_TOKEN
env_vars.description: Matrix access token for the bot account

env_vars.name: MATRIX_CRYPTO_STORE_PATH
env_vars.description: Path to persist E2EE crypto store

env_vars.name: MATRIX_RECOVERY_KEY
env_vars.description: Cross-signing recovery key for key verification bootstrapping

cli_commands.description: Start the messaging gateway including Matrix sync listener

config_keys.description: Path to persist E2EE crypto keys
Room IDs the bot responds in
Use proxy mode for E2EE (required on macOS without native libolm)
Enable MSC3245 voice message support (default: true if TTS configured)

code_examples.title: Matrix config with E2EE
code_examples.code:
matrix:
  homeserver: "https://matrix.org"
  user_id: "@hermesbot:matrix.org"
  access_token: ""
  crypto_store_path: "/data/matrix-crypto"
  recovery_key: ""
  allowed_rooms:
    - "!roomid:matrix.org"

code_examples.title: Proxy mode for macOS
code_examples.code:
matrix:
  proxy_mode: true  # use when libolm not available natively
  homeserver: "https://matrix.org"
  user_id: "@hermesbot:matrix.org"
  access_token: ""

gotchas: libolm must be installed for E2EE support on Linux; on macOS use proxy_mode: true
The crypto_store_path must persist across restarts or the bot will lose its session keys and be unable to decrypt messages
Without a recovery_key, the bot cannot verify its own cross-signing keys after a fresh start; messages may show as unverified
Room IDs look like !abc123:server.com (note the ! prefix, not #)
The bot must be invited to rooms and accept the invitation before it can receive messages
E2EE key sharing requires that the bot is verified by at least one device in the room; use the recovery key or manually verify
Sync tokens persist progress; if lost, the bot may re-process old messages on next start
full_text:
## Matrix

Connect Hermes to the Matrix protocol via the [mautrix](https://github.com/mautrix/python) SDK.

### Setup

#### 1. Create a bot account

Register a dedicated account on your Matrix homeserver (or matrix.org). Then get an access token:

```bash
curl -XPOST https://matrix.org/_matrix/client/v3/login   -d '{type:m.login.password,user:hermesbot,password:yourpassword}'
```

Copy the  from the response.

#### 2. Install libolm (for E2EE)

```bash
# Ubuntu/Debian
sudo apt-get install libolm-dev python3-olm

# macOS: libolm is not available as a native binary
# Use proxy_mode: true instead (see below)
```

#### 3. Config

```yaml
matrix:
  homeserver: "https://matrix.org"
  user_id: "@hermesbot:matrix.org"
  access_token: ""
  crypto_store_path: "/data/matrix-crypto"
  recovery_key: ""
  allowed_rooms:
    - "!roomid:matrix.org"
```

### E2EE (end-to-end encryption)

Hermes uses libolm for E2EE. The crypto store at  persists session keys — back it up. If lost, the bot will be unable to decrypt messages from existing sessions.

The  bootstraps cross-signing so the bot's keys are trusted by other devices in the room.

### Proxy mode (macOS)

On macOS, libolm is not available as a native Python package. Use proxy mode, which delegates E2EE operations to an external process:

```yaml
matrix:
  proxy_mode: true
```

### Room allowlist

Room IDs use the  prefix (not  which is the alias):

```yaml
matrix:
  allowed_rooms:
    - "!abc123xyz:matrix.org"
```

Invite the bot to a room, then it will auto-accept and start listening.

### Voice messages (MSC3245)

If TTS is configured, Hermes can send voice messages using the MSC3245 format:

```yaml
matrix:
  voice_messages: true
```

### Reactions

The bot uses Matrix reactions as delivery acknowledgments (similar to Telegram reactions).
